skill.name: global-error-handling
skill.description: Implement robust error handling with user-friendly messages, specific exception types, fail-fast validation, centralized error boundaries, graceful degradation, and proper resource cleanup. Use this skill when writing try-catch blocks, raising exceptions, handling API errors, validating input, implementing error boundaries, managing external service failures, or ensuring resource cleanup. Apply when working with error handling code in any language, exception classes, error middleware, API error responses, retry logic, or any code that needs to handle failures gracefully while maintaining system stability and providing clear feedback to users.
skill.summary: Guide coding agents to design reliable error handling, validation, cleanup, retries, and user-facing failure messages.
skill.author: EIS-ITS

security_audit.summary: Static analysis flagged relative path traversal and weak cryptography patterns in SKILL.md. AI review found no executable code, no network access, no secret access, and no confirmed malicious intent. The relative Markdown link is a minor filesystem context concern, while the weak cryptography hits are false positives from prose.

low_findings.title: Relative Path Reference in Markdown
low_findings.description: FALSE_POSITIVE for path traversal execution. SKILL.md line 29 contains a relative Markdown link to a standards document, not file-reading code. It can still direct an assistant outside the skill folder, so it remains a low filesystem context concern.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The flagged ../../../ sequence appears only inside a Markdown link. No executable file operation or data exfiltration path is present in the scanned file.

low_findings.title: Weak Cryptography Static Match False Positive
low_findings.description: FALSE_POSITIVE for weak cryptography. The flagged lines contain skill description text and normal prose about error handling, not cryptographic algorithms or crypto API usage.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The file is Markdown guidance with no code, imports, crypto calls, or hash functions. The matches are substrings in ordinary English text.

content.value_statement: Unhandled failures can confuse users and hide operational issues. This skill helps Claude, Codex, and Claude Code apply consistent error handling patterns.
actual_capabilities: Guides try-catch and exception handling decisions.
Promotes specific exception types and clear user messages.
Supports fail-fast validation for inputs and preconditions.
Advises on retry handling for transient service failures.
Covers cleanup for files, connections, locks, and similar resources.
Encourages centralized error boundaries and middleware.
limitations: Does not include runnable helper scripts or libraries.
Does not inspect live production logs or monitoring systems.
Depends on the assistant having access to the target code.

use_cases.title: Harden API Failure Handling
use_cases.description: Design consistent responses, retries, logging, and validation for service endpoints.

use_cases.title: Improve User-Facing Error Messages
use_cases.description: Replace technical failures with clear messages that explain next steps.

use_cases.title: Review Cleanup and Recovery Paths
use_cases.description: Check that resources close correctly when operations fail.

prompt_templates.title: Add Basic Error Handling
prompt_templates.prompt: Review this function and add clear error handling, specific exceptions, and user-friendly failure messages.
prompt_templates.scenario: Beginner review of a single function.

prompt_templates.title: Validate Inputs Early
prompt_templates.prompt: Update this workflow to validate inputs and preconditions before side effects happen.

prompt_templates.title: Design API Error Responses
prompt_templates.prompt: Design centralized API error handling for these routes, including status codes, logging, and safe client messages.

prompt_templates.title: Audit Resilience Strategy
prompt_templates.prompt: Audit this service for retries, graceful degradation, cleanup, and monitoring gaps across failure paths.

output_examples.input: A payment request can fail when the provider times out.
output_examples.output: The assistant recommends a short retry policy, idempotency protection, structured logging, and a clear message asking the user to try again.

output_examples.input: A file import leaves temporary files after validation errors.
output_examples.output: The assistant suggests fail-fast validation and guaranteed cleanup so temporary resources are removed after every failure path.

output_examples.input: A React screen crashes when optional profile data is missing.
output_examples.output: The assistant recommends an error boundary, safe fallback rendering, and a user message that avoids exposing internal details.

best_practices: Validate inputs before starting operations with side effects.
Use specific exception types and preserve useful diagnostic context.
Log operational details while showing users concise safe messages.
anti_patterns: Do not catch every error and continue silently.
Do not expose stack traces or secrets in user-facing messages.
Do not retry permanent validation failures or authorization errors.

faq.question: What does this skill help with?
faq.answer: It helps coding agents design consistent error handling, validation, cleanup, retries, and graceful degradation.

faq.question: Does it run code or install dependencies?
faq.answer: No. The skill is Markdown guidance and does not include executable scripts.

faq.question: Can it handle frontend error boundaries?
faq.answer: Yes. It includes guidance for React and similar application boundary patterns.

faq.question: Can it design API error responses?
faq.answer: Yes. It can guide status codes, centralized middleware, logging, and safe client messages.

faq.question: Does it replace language documentation?
faq.answer: No. Use it with the framework and language documentation for exact APIs.

faq.question: Why is the audit risk low?
faq.answer: The only real concern is a relative Markdown link. No executable dangerous behavior was found.
